text:
Long ago, in a quiet, serene forest, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. Oliver lived there alone, but he was never lonely. Every evening, you could hear him singing his enchanting songs. One autumn day, as Oliver fluttered down from his lofty perch to join the creatures munching apples off trees, he heard a tiny voice singing quietly.

There was a young fox named Luna. She lived by herself, creating wonderful arts and crafts from scraps. When Luna realized it was an old owl, she sang a cheerful song about discovering a friend in the owl.

Oliver didn't know anything about foxes, but he was intrigued. Every evening Luna visited him; they'd talk for hours about dreams and doodles. Oliver taught Luna how to hum louder. She learned owl calls.

One day, a big, handsome stag came to visit Luna. They whispered together secrets all night. The next day, the stag invited Luna into his wild woods habitat. She loved the wild
